meaning: n.土音(尤指爱尔兰人讲英语时的土腔),(旧时爱尔兰人穿的)粗革皮鞋
examples: Gill speaks in a quiet Irish brogue.
Grandfather spoke with a thick Scottish brogue.
This time I let Joe do the talking. He was Irish and he had a bit of a brogue.
Impatient, brash, and speaking in something of a Scottish brogue, Drippy is both adviser and sidekick to Oliver.
It's not every day you chance upon a Sri Lankan British singer with a Kingston-meets-cockney brogue, rapping about revolution.
